description_long: This airplane features a sleek, angular fuselage with a forward-mounted cockpit and a distinctive red and gray color scheme. The wings are sharply swept back and mounted low on the fuselage, each equipped with cylindrical engine nacelles and extended weapon-like protrusions at the tips. The tail section is compact, with a single vertical stabilizer and no visible horizontal stabilizers, giving it a unique silhouette. The surface appears metallic and weathered, with visible panel lines and markings that suggest a utilitarian, possibly military, design. The engines are integrated into the wings, and the landing gear consists of two main struts and a single nose strut, all fixed and extended. The overall design is reminiscent of a futuristic or science fiction-inspired fighter jet.
description: A futuristic, red and gray low-wing airplane with swept wings, forward cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring a weathered metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the sharply swept wings, forward cockpit, and prominent engine nacelles on each wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angular fuselage, forward cockpit canopy, and the fixed landing gear struts.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the single vertical stabilizer, engine exhausts, and the symmetry of the swept wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the same angular profile and landing gear configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and extended tip structures; no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Single vertical stabilizer with no visible horizontal stabilizers, resembling a minimalist or unconventional tail design.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main struts and a single nose strut.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy integrated into the fuselage.
